response.justification: While the candidate possesses valuable geospatial domain expertise through 15+ years building GIS and mapping systems, a critical gap exists in the primary technical requirement. C++ work ended in 2008 (18 years ago), and the past five years have focused on TypeScript/Node.js full-stack development in web and AI-agent infrastructure—the opposite of systems-level engineering. For a mid-senior role requiring immediate C++ productivity in a data pipeline context, this skill atrophy is disqualifying. The candidate's distributed-systems experience is infrastructure-adjacent but lacks demonstrated depth in fault tolerance, consistency guarantees, or data pipeline design at scale.
strengths: 15+ years of geospatial and mapping domain knowledge, including GIS platform development and navigation guidance work directly aligned with routing/tiling
Proven infrastructure and low-latency systems experience, including micro-services handling millions of requests/second and async I/O optimization
Demonstrated ability to work across multiple languages and domains; shows technical curiosity and problem-solving mindset across diverse tech stacks
concerns: C++ skills severely outdated; most recent substantial C++ work was in 2007–2008 (18 years ago), with no evidence of maintaining or using the language since
Recent 5+ years of work centered on TypeScript/Node.js web development and AI-agent tooling, not systems-level engineering or data pipeline architecture
No explicit experience with routing optimization, tile-based data structures, navigation tiling systems, or large-scale data transformation pipelines required for this role

key_factors.factor: Distributed systems and data pipeline design experience
